goal: 支持 Codex hook，并按 dogfood 思路本地安装验证

summary.title: Codex hook integration
summary.what: Implemented the Codex agent hook package, registered it in the hooks CLI, and added tests for install/uninstall, event parsing, and SessionStart context output.
summary.why: Mainline already supported Cursor hooks as a context-provider integration; Codex needed the same lifecycle support using Codex's .codex/hooks.json and codex_hooks feature flag.
summary.user_goal: 支持 Codex hook，并按 dogfood 思路本地安装验证

decisions.chose: Install SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, and Stop hooks and map them to Mainline session_start, turn_start, and turn_end lifecycle events.
decisions.rationale: These are enough to provide startup context and lifecycle webhook signals without expanding hook responsibilities.

decisions.chose: Use Codex's hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext from SessionStart with the existing dispatcher-rendered Mainline context snapshot.
decisions.rationale: This reuses the Cursor-tested context-provider path while respecting Codex's host protocol.

decisions.chose: Write repo-local .codex/hooks.json and ensure .codex/config.toml has [features] codex_hooks = true while preserving unrelated user content.
decisions.rationale: Codex hooks are opt-in per project and require the feature flag before hooks are read.

decisions.chose: Keep bare mainline hooks install defaulting to Cursor; Codex is selected with --agent codex or --all.
decisions.rationale: Avoid surprising existing users who adopted the first Cursor-only flow.

rejected.alternative: Add semantic start/append/seal automation to Codex hooks.
rejected.reason: The hooks subsystem was intentionally reduced to context provider plus observer signals; semantic decisions stay with the agent.
.codex/ is ignored and installation is a per-developer opt-in artifact.

rejected.alternative: Change the default install agent from Cursor to all agents.
rejected.reason: That would alter established behavior for existing Cursor users.

risks: Codex hook schema may evolve; tests cover the documented JSON shapes used here but future Codex releases could add or rename hook fields.
Install modifies repo-local .codex/config.toml and hooks install still enables Mainline's [hooks] toggle in .mainline/config.toml as an opt-in side effect.
followups: Run a real new Codex conversation after local install to confirm Codex injects the SessionStart additionalContext into the model context, beyond the manual dispatch smoke test.

architectural_claims: Agent-specific hook protocols stay isolated in internal/hooks/agents/<agent> packages while the shared dispatcher remains agent-agnostic.
Codex hooks follow the existing context-provider architecture and do not perform semantic workflow decisions.
behavioral_changes: mainline hooks list-agents now includes codex with session-start, user-prompt-submit, and stop events.
mainline hooks install --agent codex writes .codex/hooks.json command hooks and enables codex_hooks in .codex/config.toml.
mainline hooks codex session-start returns Codex-compatible JSON with additional Mainline context.
mainline hooks codex user-prompt-submit and stop parse Codex stdin payloads into normalized lifecycle events.

data_model_changes.name: Codex hook groups
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Created only when the user installs Codex hooks.

data_model_changes.name: codex_hooks feature flag
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Set to true by mainline hooks install --agent codex.

security_implications: Hook commands execute the configured mainline binary path; install uses the existing --bin fail-soft wrapper pattern and only removes commands containing Mainline-managed markers.
Unrelated user hook commands in .codex/hooks.json are preserved.
migration_notes: Existing Cursor hook behavior and default install target are unchanged.
Users opt into Codex support with mainline hooks install --agent codex.
